error.regex: (Incompatible shapes|InvalidArgumentError.*shape|logits and labels must have the same|Dimensions must be equal)

verdict.summary: Tensor shapes do not match. Common with loss functions where model output and label dimensions differ.

dead_ends.action: Reshape labels to match model output
dead_ends.why_fails: If model outputs 10 classes but you have 5, reshape just masks the real problem: wrong model or wrong labels

dead_ends.action: Use sparse_categorical_crossentropy to avoid shape issues
dead_ends.why_fails: Only works if labels are integer indices, not one-hot. Using it with one-hot labels gives wrong results.

workarounds.action: Match loss function to label format
workarounds.how: Integer labels: sparse_categorical_crossentropy. One-hot labels: categorical_crossentropy.

workarounds.action: Verify model output shape matches number of classes
workarounds.how: model.summary()  # check last layer output shape

workarounds.action: Check dataset label shapes before training
workarounds.how: print(y_train.shape, y_train[:5])  # verify dimensions and format
